blocks.text: defineConfig is the central place to configure the API layer — caching, auth, roles, and rate limiting. It returns a typed defineApi helper and a config object to pass to fsApiRoutes.
Pass the config to fsApiRoutes so the builder can crawl and register all .api.ts files.
app.fsClientRoutes() registers all pages, layouts, and the app wrapper from the file system. app.fsApiRoutes() registers all .api.ts files and the OpenAPI spec.
HowlBuilder wraps esbuild to bundle islands, apply CSS modules, and run the dev server with hot reload.

# Build
deno run -A dev.ts build

# Run the compiled server
deno run -A dist/compiled-entry.js

# Override host/port via env
DENO_PORT=9000 DENO_HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 deno run -A dist/compiled-entry.js
The production build outputs a single compiled-entry.js. No node_modules, no build tools needed at runtime — just Deno.
compiled-entry.js coerces DENO_PORT to a number and throws on a non-numeric value, so a typo fails fast at startup instead of silently picking a random port.

app.handler() is built lazily on first call and cached per listener. app.listen() spawns the main listener plus one secondary listener per WS port — each gets its own cached handler with the same middleware pipeline.

Once handler() has been built, the app is frozen: app.use(...), app.get/post/..., app.fsApiRoutes(...), app.layout(...), app.mountApp(...) all throw with a clear error. Register everything before requesting the handler.
